Patch Set 4: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

The removal of:

 -#ifdef EXT3_MB_HINT_GROUP_ALLOC
 -                ext3_mb_discard_inode_preallocations(inode);
 -#endif

Is incorrect. EXT3_MB_HINT_GROUP_ALLOC is always set, so the discard line should be kept.

Patch Set 4: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Doh! I deleted one too many lines Andreas. New patch coming with this fix plus I removed the journal callback to avoid colliding with the patch from LU-1199 this time around.

(1 inline comment)

Patch Set 5:

Chris I looked into what you suggested and found it would takes a lot more surgery to make it work. Yes you can move the lustre macros that are specific for ext4 to ldiskfs-build.m4 but then you run into the issue of the config.h for ldiskfs conflicting with the config.h for lustre itself. You can rename ldiskfs config.h to ldiskfs-config.h and include it but then you run into the issue of PACKAGE_* being redefined. To get around that then you have to do zfs meta type magic to make it work. At that point is it worth the effort especially if the fsfilt layer is going away soon.

Patch Set 5:

I did not suggest trying to include ldiskfs's config.h from lustre.  As you have learned, one should never attempt to export config.h to another package.

You do, however, need to have ldiskfs export the information in SOME reasonable way.  I created the infrastructure that would make this easy for you to export a new variable in Patch Set 2 of http://review.whamcloud.com/5005 (you really need to look at 2, patch set 3 does not have any of this).

Andreas mark that down for the complexity, largely, I think, due to the lack of consumers of the information.  Perhaps now that you _have_ a consumer that approach could be resurrected.

The key part was the creation of lustre_definitions.h.in.  That is a header that was intended to be used by both ldiskfs, and more importantly installed as part of the future ldiskfs-devel package so that lustre can easily check which features ldiskfs offers.

Patch Set 5:

James, are you saying also that this problem will go away when fsfilt is removed?  Will the ldiskfs osd not need to call *ext_pblock when fsfilt is removed?

If we only need a very short term fix, you might just add an ldiskfs patch to reintroduce ext_pblock into ldiskfs for the newer ext4 versions.  Just have it wrap ext4_ext_pblock() and export ext_pblock.

Then there would be no need to change the lustre code to handle the new call.

Patch Set 5:

Yes that is correct that once fsfilt is gone then this goes away. I'm tending to thing also that its better to create a rhel6 specific ldiskfs patch to handle this.
Uploaded patch set 6.

Patch Set 6:

This patch is a place holder so SLES11 SP2 testing can continue until a proper solution is create.

parents.subject: LU-1812 fsfilt: ext4_free_blocks() has changed slightly

commit.subject: LU-1812 fsfilt: ext_pblock renamed to ext4_ext_pblock
commit.message:
LU-1812 fsfilt: ext_pblock renamed to ext4_ext_pblock

For kernels 2.6.35 and above ext_pblock was renamed to
ext4_ext_pblock. With no more RHEL5 kernel support for
ldiskfs we also clean up more macros.

see kernel commit bf89d16f6ef5389f1b9d8046e838ec87b9b3f8b9
for pblock change.

Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: chas williams - CONTRACTOR <chas@cmf.nrl.navy.mil>
Change-Id: I3ce7f27f6fd6826380e6f2f54b2d50d09d36f78a
